reforms.description: Estonia has established an applied centre to enhance cooperation between the public and private sectors in R&D, aiming to foster collaboration between businesses and academia.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration between academia and industry, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The applied centre has been established to facilitate R&D cooperation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the applied centre represents a significant step towards improving R&D collaboration, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in Estonia.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have established an applied centre to bring business and academia together.

reforms.description: Estonia plans to further digitalise the public administration to enhance efficiency and service delivery.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Digitalisation can improve public sector efficiency, leading to better service delivery and potentially higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set out plans to further digitalise the public administration.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards modernising public services, which can enhance overall productivity and service quality.
reforms.source_quote: The government is working on further digitalising the public administration.

reforms.description: The Estonian Business and Innovation Agency offers grants to cover the costs of digital diagnostics, aimed at enhancing the digital capabilities of firms. However, the program is currently limited to firms with annual sales above EUR 200,000, which restricts access for many SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to digital tools, the program aims to enhance productivity and competitiveness among firms, particularly SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The program is currently operational, providing grants to eligible firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the program is significant in supporting digital transformation, its current limitations on eligibility reduce its overall impact on the broader SME sector.
reforms.source_quote: The Estonian Business and Innovation Agency offers grants to cover the costs of digital diagnostics.

reforms.description: Estonia is committed to allocating at least 1% of GDP annually to public research, with a quarter earmarked for the private sector, aiming to enhance R&D activities and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public funding for R&D is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities are committed to allocating at least 1% of GDP annually to public research.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to public R&D funding, which is crucial for fostering innovation and enhancing the competitiveness of the Estonian economy.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities are committed to allocating at least 1% of GDP annually to public research, with around a quarter earmarked for the private sector.

reforms.description: A new centre for applied research aims to enhance collaboration between the business and research sectors, promoting innovation and R&D investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating collaboration between businesses and research institutions is expected to enhance innovation and R&D outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: A new centre for applied research aims to bring together the business and research sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it seeks to bridge the gap between academia and industry, fostering innovation through collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: A new centre for applied research aims to bring together the business and research sectors, which is welcome.

reforms.description: Estonia is planning to develop demand-driven mobility over 2023-26 to improve public transport efficiency and accessibility, particularly in rural areas where fixed routes are insufficient.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing public transport can reduce emissions and improve accessibility, contributing to overall economic efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is planning to develop demand-driven mobility over 2023-26.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift towards more efficient public transport, which is crucial for reducing emissions and improving urban mobility.
reforms.source_quote: The government is planning to develop demand-driven mobility over 2023-26.

reforms.description: Estonia plans to invest EUR 361.5 million to achieve full electrification of its rail network by 2028, supported by EU Structural Funds and ETS revenue.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Electrifying the rail network will enhance transport efficiency and reduce emissions, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Estonia plans to invest EUR 361.5 million to achieve full electrification by 2028.
reforms.importance_rationale: This investment is significant for modernizing the rail infrastructure and reducing transport emissions, which is vital for sustainable development.
reforms.source_quote: Estonia plans to invest EUR 361.5 million to achieve full electrification by 2028.

reforms.description: Estonia is expanding public R&D investment and increasing funding for environment-related issues to encourage low-carbon technology innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D investment is expected to enhance innovation in low-carbon technologies, which can lead to sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to expand public R&D investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding priorities towards green technology, which is crucial for addressing climate change and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Further encourage low-carbon technology innovation by expanding public R&D investment and by increasing the share of funding on environment-related issues.

reforms.description: Estonia has expanded dental care benefits for essential services to all insured adults, increasing the public insurance coverage from EUR 40 to EUR 60 per year in 2024, aimed at reducing out-of-pocket payments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to dental care, the reform enhances overall health outcomes, which can lead to a more productive workforce and lower healthcare costs in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The limit for dental care benefits has been increased to EUR 60 per year for 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant enhancement of existing health benefits, addressing financial barriers to essential dental care for insured adults.
reforms.source_quote: In 2024, this limit has increased to EUR 60 per year.

reforms.description: Reforms in 2018 automated the administration of pharmaceutical benefits through a centralized e-prescription system, lowering the eligibility threshold for annual co-payments from EUR 300 to EUR 100.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The automation of pharmaceutical benefits improves efficiency and access, reducing out-of-pocket expenses and enhancing public health outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The administration of benefits was automated through the e-prescription system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the efficiency of the healthcare system, improving access to medications for the population.
reforms.source_quote: Reforms in 2018 lowered the eligibility threshold for annual co-payments from EUR 300 to EUR 100.
